title: AAP vs Delhi HC Judge: Kejriwal-Sisodia courtroom showdown explained in 10 points
content: Delhi High Court rejected Arvind Kejriwal's plea to recuse Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma from the excise policy case, citing lack of evidence for bias claims. Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia have now refused to appear before the judge, escalating the legal and political standoff. The court warned against forum shopping and undermining judicial independence.

title: ‘Scam Altman and Greg Stockman’: Elon Musk mocks OpenAI CEO and co-founder
content: Elon Musk has reignited his legal battle against OpenAI, accusing founders Sam Altman and Greg Brockman of "looting a charity." Musk claims they enriched themselves and betrayed the company's non-profit mission. He seeks $150 billion in damages, arguing this precedent could harm all US charitable giving. OpenAI counters, calling the suit a jealous distraction from Musk's rival AI venture, xAI.

title: Will upside in gold be capped due to rising oil prices? Check outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Any upside in gold prices is likely to be capped by gains in crude oil prices, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: 0% tariffs, visas, $20 bn investment: How India benefits in trade deal with NZ
content: India and New Zealand have signed a 'once-in-a-generation' trade pact, granting Indian exporters duty-free access to New Zealand. The agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $5 billion in five years and includes a $20 billion investment commitment from New Zealand. Both nations are optimistic about the pact's potential to boost economic growth and cooperation across various sectors.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ misses Rs 1780 crore mark worldwide by whisker
content: Dhurandhar 2 experienced a major box office drop on its 40th day. The film earned Rs 1.05 crore in India and Rs 0.25 crore overseas. Its worldwide gross collection reached Rs 1779.02 crore. Competitors Bhooth Bangla and Michael also saw reduced earnings on Monday. The movie's performance reflects a decline after its initial surge.

title: Vinesh alleges ‘deliberate attempt’ by WFI to block return after 20 months
content: Vinesh Phogat has accused the Wrestling Federation of India of “deliberately” blocking her comeback by not allowing her to register for the National Open Ranking tournament, crucial for Asian Games selection. She said the portal closed early and officials didn’t respond. WFI denied the claims, saying no one is being stopped. Vinesh fears missing the event could hurt her chances of returning to top-level competition.

title: Hafiz Saeed's close aide, Sheikh Yousaf Afridi, shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Pakistan
content: Lashkar-e-Taiba operative Sheikh Yousaf Afridi, a close aide of Hafiz Saeed, was shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. Afridi, considered a key figure in LeT's regional structure, was killed in Landi Kotal. The incident follows a recent attack on another LeT leader, Amir Hamza.

title: Disney, Warner Brothers Discovery & others to EU: Upcoming act should target Big Tech
content: Major European media companies are urging EU officials to reconsider a new law. They fear the Digital Fairness Act, aimed at online consumer protection, could unintentionally damage their businesses. The companies argue that existing regulations already cover their operations. They want the focus to be on large tech firms where responsibility gaps exist, not on established media sectors.

title: Blast near railway track in Patiala triggers panic, dismembered body found at scene
content: A low-intensity blast near a railway track in Patiala late Monday night has sparked panic. An unidentified, dismembered body was found nearby, though its connection to the explosion is under investigation. The blast caused minor damage to the freight train line. Forensic teams are examining the scene for evidence.

title: What killed Mumbai family of four? Doctors dismiss watermelon scare; probe shifts to toxins
content: Following tragic family deaths in Bhendi Bazaar, doctors reassured the public that watermelons are safe, emphasizing their high water content and hydrating properties. Experts stated that even stale watermelons are unlikely to be fatal, drawing a distinction from rare incidents involving bitter cucumbers. There is no need for widespread panic regarding this popular summer fruit.

title: 'Nobody else should be allowed': Gavaskar urges BCCI action over fresh IPL concern
content: Sunil Gavaskar criticised long IPL match durations, blaming frequent on-field entry of reserve players and staff for delays. Despite BCCI penalties, games often exceed four hours. He called such scenes “unnecessary” and urged stricter limits on field access. Gavaskar also stressed discipline, citing Richie Benaud, and suggested reducing batter readiness time and enforcing penalties to speed up play.

title: AHSEC Assam results released: Direct link to download class 12 scorecards here
content: Assam Higher Secondary (HS) Result 2026 is out today, April 28, for Class 12 students. Over 3 lakh students can check their scorecards online via official websites, DigiLocker, and the UPOLOBDHA app.  The examinations were held from February 11 to March 16. Students need their roll number or name to access results.

title: Indian-origin man jailed in Singapore for molesting minor
content: An Indian-origin man in Singapore has been sentenced to 19 months in jail for molesting his friend's sister when she was a child. The victim, who was between seven and nine years old at the time, reported the abuse over a decade later. The man contested the charges but was convicted on two counts of molestation.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: Internet fumes after Manu Bhaker asked about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: At an NRAI event in Delhi, Manu Bhaker was asked about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, sparking online criticism for shifting focus from her Olympic achievements. Bhaker emphasized mentorship, saying age doesn’t limit talent. Social media, including Joy Bhattacharjya, called the question unfair and cricket-centric. Meanwhile, Sooryavanshi continues to shine, becoming the youngest to 1,000 T20 runs and emerging as a promising future star for India.

title: 'All good people left Kejriwal': Rijiju hits back at 'coercion' charge as 7 RS MPs join BJP
content: Kiren Rijiju refuted claims of coercion as seven Rajya Sabha MPs switched from AAP to BJP, stating "good people" have left Arvind Kejriwal. He accused Kejriwal of running AAP as a "private party." Rijiju also criticised the INDIA bloc, alleging internal rifts and a lack of principles, while backing West Bengal's electoral roll revision ahead of polls.

title: 'I hate all Indian bowlers': Pak batter's controversial remark sparks row - Watch
content: Hasan said he feels extra motivation while playing against India and wants to attack their bowlers. He also said he dislikes every bowler in the Indian team.

title: Iran running out of space to store its crude; is the real financial crisis still coming?
content: Iran faces a critical storage crunch, with only weeks of capacity left, potentially forcing production cuts of 1.5 million barrels per day. Despite a sharp drop in oil exports due to a naval blockade, the immediate financial impact on Tehran may be delayed for several months.

title: 'No hope for justice': Day after Kejriwal, Sisodia pens letter to Delhi HC judge Swarana Kanta
content: AAP leaders Manish Sisodia and Arvind Kejriwal have declared they will not appear in proceedings before Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma in the Delhi High Court's excise policy case. Citing a lack of hope for justice and potential conflict of interest, they are opting for Satyagraha instead of legal representation.

title: Trump, Melania call on ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel — what triggered the row
content: Jimmy Kimmel faced backlash for calling Melania Trump an "expectant widow" days before a shooting near the White House Correspondents' Dinner. Melania Trump and Donald Trump condemned the remark as hateful and violent, with Trump calling for Kimmel's firing. White House Press Secretary also criticized the "deranged" rhetoric.

title: Salim Dola, key Dawood Ibrahim aide linked to Rs 5,000 crore drug empire, deported to India
content: A major win for Indian security! High-ranking drug mafia operative Salim Dola, a Dawood Ibrahim associate, has been deported from Istanbul. Orchestrating a multi-crore synthetic drug empire, Dola's capture and return to Delhi under heavy guard signifies a significant blow to the D-Company's international network. He is now undergoing intensive interrogation.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: Cowboys send Pickens to Bills for Coleman, picks
content: The Dallas Cowboys face a crucial decision regarding wide receiver George Pickens. CBS Sports suggests a trade. ClutchPoints suggest Pickens could be acquired in exchange for draft capital and wide receiver Keon Coleman. However, recent reports indicate the Buffalo Bills are committed to WR Keon Coleman. This alters the proposed trade scenario and leaving Pickens’ future with Dallas uncertain. But he wants a trade. So, we are back to square one.

title: Fact check: Clavicular pregnancy rumour after 10-day romance debunked
content: Clavicular, whose real name is Braden Peters, went viral after claims he is expecting a child with a girlfriend of 10 days. The rumor came from a viral post and a misunderstood livestream. There is no proof of pregnancy. The story is false, while real concerns remain about his health, recent hospital stay, and ongoing controversies.

title: 'I cried harder' over painful parenting moment with daughter: Serena Williams
content: Serena Williams shared a real parenting moment where she had to discipline her daughter, even though it made them both cry. She stood by her decision after her daughter missed a sleepover for not following bedtime rules. The story shows that discipline is hard but important. Serena Williams continues to balance motherhood with honesty and strong values.

title: ‘Best time to buy a gun… today’: Chilling online trail of suspect behind Trump dinner shooting
content: Social media activity of Cole Tomas Allen, charged in an alleged assassination attempt on President Trump, shifted from gaming to angry political messages. Posts from 2024 and 2025 criticized Trump, questioned election outcomes, and encouraged gun ownership, mirroring a message sent to family before the incident.

title: Putin ally’s $500 million Russian superyacht sails through Hormuz despite US blockade: Report
content: A Russian-linked superyacht, Nord, owned by sanctioned billionaire Alexey Mordashov, has successfully sailed through the Strait of Hormuz. This strategic waterway remains a flashpoint amid ongoing tensions, with maritime traffic significantly reduced. The yacht's passage highlights its defiance of sanctions and the complex geopolitical landscape.

title: King Charles III to address US Congress amid Iran war tensions: What to expect
content: King Charles III will address a joint session of the US Congress, calling for "reconciliation and renewal" in UK-US ties amid strains over the Iran war. The monarch's rare speech aims to reinforce the long-standing alliance, acknowledging political differences while stressing unity against global challenges and shared democratic values.

title: Oil prices climb towards $110: Brent, WTI continue to rise as US-Iran peace talks stall
content: Oil prices surged Tuesday, extending Monday's gains as stalled US-Iran peace efforts and Strait of Hormuz disruptions pressured market sentiment. WTI Crude and Brent Crude saw notable increases, driven by constrained Middle East oil flows and ongoing geopolitical tensions. Investor focus remains on diplomatic developments and central bank policy signals.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock market recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Lupin, Jio Financial Services, Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone are the top stocks that Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research, Mirae Asset ShareKhan recommends for buying on April 28, 2026.

title: Maharashtra minister Nitesh Rane awarded 1-mth jail for 2019 mud attack on NHAI engineer
content: Maharashtra minister Nitesh Rane received a one-month jail sentence for pouring mud on an NHAI engineer in 2019. The court, while acquitting 29 others, found Rane guilty of intentional insult, emphasizing lawmakers shouldn't take the law into their own hands. His sentence was later suspended for appeal.

title: 'If anyone can challenge Virat, it's ... ': Kohli's record called 'stepping stone'
content: Virat Kohli became the first batter to cross 9,000 IPL runs, further cementing his legacy of consistency and dominance. Irfan Pathan said the milestone is “just a stepping stone” for Kohli. At Arun Jaitley Stadium, Kohli played an unbeaten, match-winning knock. With eight centuries, strong form, and Orange Cap contention, he remains central to RCB’s success this season and their strong playoff push.

title: Forest fire prevention measures intensified in Erode Division
content: Officials said technology is being deployed for early detection of fires, along with the installation of fire watch towers and training for field staff and local communities on fire prevention practices

title: Chandrababu Naidu lays foundation for Google’s ₹1.35 lakh crore AI data centre in Visakhapatnam
content: The 1-Gigawatt hyperscale AI data centre, coming up on 601.4 acres at Tarluvada at an investment of ₹1.35 lakh crore, is one of the largest single FDI projects in India and forms part of Google’s $15 billion commitment to Andhra Pradesh

title: Trinamool’s ‘nepo babies’ face tough contest in 2026 Assembly election
content: Trio includes Shrreya Pande, daughter of late Minister Sadhan Pande from Maniktala; Sirsanya Banerjee, son of MP Kalyan Banerjee from Uttarpara; and Tirthankar Ghosh, son of chief whip Nirmal Ghosh from Panihati

title: Caught in unrest after Zubeen Garg’s death, Assam’s Tezpur university starts afresh
content: The central Tezpur University is offering a bunch of new courses and is in the process of acquiring land for two expansive campuses

title: Contract employee arrested for downloading, watching leaked ‘Jana Nayagan’ at Chennai’s vote-counting centre
content: During poll duty hours, the employee – working in the CCTV room – allegedly downloaded and watched the unreleased film illegally on his laptop, took a photograph of it, and posted it on his Facebook account around 11 p.m. on April 23

title: SCO summit: There must not be any double standards in combating terrorism, says Rajnath Singh
content: The Defence Minister said India demonstrated its “firm resolve” during Operation Sindoor that “terror epicentres are no longer immune to justifiable punishment”

title: Five-bed heatstroke ward opened at Erode government hospital
content: The ward will cater to patients suffering from symptoms such as fainting and giddiness; Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) is also being provided

title: Thane security guards stabbed: Prime facie ‘lone wolf’ attack by radicalised man, say police
content: One of the victims claimed the accused asked both of them their religion and to recite the ‘Kalma’, and when they could not, he stabbed them with a knife

title: Man killed in suspected elephant attack in Kerala’s Malappuram
content: According to local residents, he was found seriously injured in a forest-fringe area on Tuesday morning and was rushed to a private hospital in Perinthalmanna, where he later succumbed to his injuries

title: CAPFs go ‘all out’ to win ‘trust’, foster ‘peace’ ahead of last phase of Bengal polls
content: Over 100 armoured and anti-riot vehicles deployed across sensitive areas to instil confidence among voters, says official; security presence has “fostered trust and an atmosphere of peace”, says CRPF

title: Watch: Perarivalan joins Madras High Court as lawyer
content: A.G. Perarivalan, once a life convict in the assassination of Rajiv Gandhi, is now a practicing lawyer at the Madras High Court

title: Kerala hartal: Kannur sees sporadic protests, life largely unaffected
content: Police remove protesters who attempted to block vehicles, including in Kannur town, where activists tried to disrupt traffic

title: AAP MLA Mehraj Malik released from Kathua jail after J&K HC quashes his PSA detention
content: Mehraj Malik said now that he had been released, he would continue to raise and talk about the issues of the people

title: Government committed to ‘Act East, Act Fast’ for India’s ‘Ashtalakshmi’ in northeast: PM Modi
content: PM Modi, while addressing the closing ceremony of celebrations to mark the golden jubilee of Sikkim’s statehood, said his government’s focus is on boosting its connectivity, which would increase tourist inflow

title: Kerala actor Shiyas Kareem booked on cheating, intimidation charges after complaint by woman business partner
content: According to the complainant, Shiyas Kareem allegedly cheated the woman of lakhs of rupees after promising her a partnership in a new business venture. She also alleged he failed to honour the deal after receiving the money

title: Assembly election 2026 LIVE: Puducherry records highest turnout among third gender electors at 91.81%, says Election Commission
content: According to the data shared by the Election Commission between April 9 and April 23, Puducherry had 139 third gender electors, of whom 91.81 per cent exercised their franchise.

title: Speak Marathi to stay in driver’s seat: Maharashtra Transport Minister Pratap Sarnaik
content: Maharashtra Transport Minister Pratap Sarnaik stated that if one wishes to conduct business in Maharashtra, a working knowledge of Marathi is necessary

title: BJP’s rapid rise in Assam is more pronounced in ST seats
content: The 2026 results will reveal if the BJP will retain its ST-seat stronghold or concede ground to the Congress that is trying to bounce back

title: Will AAP MPs face disqualification after joining BJP?
content: Is there a law against defection? What are the exceptions to it and how has the Supreme Court interpreted them? What are experts’ view on seven of AAP’s 10 Rajya Sabha members joining the BJP last week?

title: PM Modi plays football with boys, girls in Sikkim
content: The Prime Minister shared on social media a few photographs in which he is seen playing soccer with youngsters

title: Tamil Nadu does it again with double-digit economic growth
content: The State registers 10.83% real economic growth rate during 2025-26, according to the recently-released data of the Union Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation

title: 13 Indian fishermen from Nagapattinam attacked, robbed of their catch mid-sea; one suspected Sri Lankan fisherman nabbed
content: The Sri Lankan fisherman, identified as Ayanthan of  Kankesanthurai, was captured by some Nagapattinam fishermen. He was handed over to the Department of Fisheries

title: Vote counting underway for Gujarat local body polls
content: Counting of votes began at 9 a.m. on Tuesday (April 28, 2026), State Election Commission (SEC) officials said

title: HC asks govt. to come up with rehabilitation scheme for trans persons at the taluk level
content: It asks the government to undo, as far as possible, the historical wrongs inflicted on trans persons, secure for them a life of dignity, equality, and meaningful inclusion as equal participants in the social order, and implement welfare measures for them to attain full development

title: Why quotas alone won’t increase women’s representation
content: While reservation can address the issue of underrepresentation, its impact is likely to be shaped by existing inequalities related to class, caste etc

title: Hot Westerly winds set to increase temperatures from May 1 in Chennai
content: However, this rise in temperature in coastal regions will be brought down by the daily onset of the sea breeze that will bring some respite for the coastal areas, says a senior meteorologist

title: Kerala BDS student death: Statewide hartal by Dalit, Adivasi groups disrupts life
content: Protesters blocked traffic, disrupted bus services, and forced shop closures across many areas. In some parts of the State, they also attempted to interfere with the functioning of nationalised banks. Passengers travelling to hospitals were reportedly asked to show medical records to be allowed free passage

title: Failure to change caste names of localities: T.N. Commission for Scheduled Castes seeks reports from election officials
content: Residents of an area in the Srivilliputhur Assembly constituency boycotted the election to protest the government’s failure to remove the caste name

title: Kerala’s out-of-pocket expense in hospitals still among the highest in country
content: Despite expanded insurance coverage, healthcare expenses have remained high in the State, a persistent finding across multiple National Sample Survey rounds for Kerala

title: Death of BDS student: Hartal called on April 28 unlikely to disrupt normal life in Kerala
content: Hartal conveners said vehicles would not be forcibly stopped, though they appealed to the public to cooperate by avoiding the use of private vehicles. The clarification came amid concerns over disruption to transport and trade

title: First woman MLA, MP, Minister, CM, PM and President were from Congress, not BJP, says Karnataka Minister Laxmi Hebbalkar
content: BJP misleading nation on women’s reservation to pass delimitation bill, says Karnataka Minister Laxmi Hebbalkar

title: KEM Hospital renaming controversy: Symbolism or Services?
content: A fresh controversy has been brewing with the renaming of KEM Hospital, established on January 22, 1926

title: An  IT professional turned upcycling artist in Kerala is teaching India to rethink waste
content: Using textile waste and natural dyes, Amal S Pillai’s practice merges design, sustainability and community learning

title: U.S. Pacific Air Forces chief’s visit to India cements defence ties
content: While boosting Indo-Pacific defence ties, a major focus of the visit was advancing logistics cooperation, a statement by the American Embassy says

title: 8th cohort of NSR Pre-doc successfully concludes one-year fellowship at IIM Bengaluru
content: The NSR Pre-doc is designed to create meaningful career pathways for candidates from historically under-represented communities in management and allied disciplines

title: Good prices help Kerala’s Muthalamada mango farmers offset crop losses
content: Most of the 500-plus farmers in Muthalamada, both big and small, made some profit because there was early demand for their mangoes in north Indian markets

title: Jharkhand woman found dead in rented house in Bengaluru
content: The incident came to light after neighbours noticed a strong foul smell emanating from her house and alerted the police

title: India, New Zealand ink “historic” free trade agreement, to be implemented later this year
content: The FTA, negotiations for which were announced in March 2025 and concluded in December 2025, is one of the fastest to have been negotiated by India

title: Indian-origin man sentenced in Singapore for molesting minor
content: The prosecution had sought 20 to 22 months’ jail for Singh, saying he had exploited a young victim and abused the trust placed in him by the victim and her family members

title: Odisha tribal produces skeleton of his sister’s body as proof to claim the money in her account
content: Authorities in the Patna block assured that Mr. Munda would be helped in securing a legal heir certificate and other documents required for claiming the money in the account

title: ‘Basava Sri’ award for H.D. Deve Gowda
content: Mr. Gowda apologised for not being able to participate in the Basava Jayanti programme to receive the award owing to his health

title: M.P. Assembly passes resolution to implement women’s reservation after delimitation amid uproar by Opposition
content: The Opposition members walked out of the House after Speaker Narendra Singh Tomar declined to take up the bill, ruling that a government resolution on the issue — scheduled to be moved by Chief Minister Mohan Yadav — had already been listed

title: Haryana Assembly passes women empowerment resolution; Congress boycotts session
content: Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini said that the constitution of the women empowerment committee would strengthen the women in line with the vision of Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: Citizens, NGOs step in to quench the thirst of urban animals
content: Animal welfare activists in the city have taken initiatives to ensure stray animals have access to water and are raising awareness about it as well

title: No rest for the wicked: Even as Tamil Nadu comes down from its election high, tensions are still rising
content: The issues vary from the advisability of Chief Minister M.K.Stalin taking a holiday in Kodaikkanal to the comparative performance of Tamil Nadu with other southern States with respect to the IT sector to the possibility of a coalition regime getting formed due to the presence of the Vijay-led TVK

title: US Blockade Chokes Iran Oil Trade, Vessels Pile Up Near Chabahar Port
content: Some 155 million barrels of Iranian crude are either in transit or floating storage all over the world, according to an estimate from Vortexa Ltd.

title: Husband, Wife Behind Sanctioned China Oil Firm Lose $1.4 Billion
content: The move targeted Hengli Group for allegedly facilitating billions of dollars in purchases from Tehran and injects fresh tension into an expected summit between Trump and Xi Jinping next month.

title: 'Lone Wolf, Wanted To Join ISIS': Cops On Mumbai Man Who Stabbed Guards
content: Investigators believed that during his isolation, the accused Zaib Zuber Ansari became radicalised through the internet.

title: India Now World's 5th Top Military Spender: Where China, Pakistan Stand
content: As per SIPRI report, India is gradually shifting away from Russia and buying more from France, Israel and the United States.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Two Bengals, North vs South
content: The divide between north and south Bengal is not merely geographic. It is the gap between two theories of what politics is for. And on the 4th of May, one of those theories will be proven right.

title: Burqa, Malaysia: Fresh Details On How Accused Tried To Convert TCS Staff
content: Nida Khan has been charged with hurting the survivor's religious sentiments and intimidating her to change her religion, said sources.

title: Iran Ready To Reopen Hormuz On 3 Conditions, Trump Unlikely To Accept Them
content: It is not clear why Trump is not satisfied with the proposal, but the Iranian proposal would push negotiations on the country's nuclear programme to a later date. Trump has repeatedly insisted that Iran cannot have nuclear weapons.

title: How A Sanctioned "Floating Gas Station" Helped Iran Transfer Oil
content: The vessel, identified as the M/T Tifani, was carrying Iranian crude when it was intercepted, according to a statement from the Pentagon.

title: Amravati 2.0 In Kolhapur: Man, 22, Sexually Exploits Women In Maharashtra
content: The Kolhapur case comes days after police arrested Mohammad Ayaz alias Tanveer in Amravati, who is accused of sexually exploiting at least 180 minor girls and recording more than 350 obscene videos.

title: Manish Sisodia Refuses To Pursue Liquor Policy Case In Delhi Judge's Court
content: Arvind Kejriwal decided to neither appear in person or appoint any lawyer for his case in Justice Sharma's court.

title: Andhra Pradesh Journalist, Out On Morning Walk, Chased And Stabbed To Death
content: Jaganmohan Reddy, a journalist associated with ABN Andhra Jyothy, was murdered in Chittoor, home district of Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu.

title: "Go Back To England": Argentina's Vice President Targets Falkland Residents
content: The remark came after a leaked memo from the Pentagon suggested the US might reconsider its stance on Britain’s claim to the territory

title: Madhya Pradesh Fast-Tracks Uniform Civil Code Push, Forms High-Level Panel
content: The move comes weeks after NDTV, on April 9, had reported that Chief Minister Dr Mohan Yadav-led government was actively preparing to move on UCC.

title: What Goldman Sachs Predicts About Future Of Strait Of Hormuz
content: Even though both US and Iran have stopped major attacks, including ballistic missiles and drones, the conflict hasn't truly ended.

title: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Lays Foundation For $15 Billion Google AI Data Centre
content: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu lays foundation for $15B Google AI data centre near Visakhapatnam.

title: Delivery Agent, 24, Found Dead In Trunk, Killed By Wife's Lover, Friend
content: During interrogation, the alleged accused told the police that he was involved in an illicit relationship with the victim's wife

title: Pics: PM Modi Plays Football With "Young Friends" In Gangtok
content: Gangtok, April 28 (IANS) Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Tuesday joined his "young friends" in playing football in Gangtok, Sikkim, calling it a lovely morning and an "energising session".

title: Only 40% Freshers Secure Rs 5 Lakh Package, 90% Ready To Work At Lower CTC
content: A new study has found that while 73% of undergraduate freshers expect salaries above Rs 5 lakh per annum, only 40% actually secure it.

title: Last supper: No chicken for dinner, Telangana man shouts at wife, she kills him with sickle
content: the victim was identified as Kodandam Shivaji from Gosangi Colony . the 28-year-old scrap vendor was attacked with a sickle after he shouted at his wife for not cooking chicken . he collapsed after the assault and sustained a head injury .

title: 'Why live with him before marriage?' SC says quitting live-in relationship not criminal offence
content: a bench of justices said it could not allow her plea for a criminal case of sexual harassment and exploitation against a woman who was in a live-in relationship for 15 years . the court said there was no legal binding in such a relationship and that people should be careful about the vagaries .
